statement.p: An object is launched upward at the height of 400 meters. Its height can be modeled by h=-4.9t^2+100t+400, where h stands for the objects height in meters, and t stands for time passed in seconds since its launch. The objects height will be 420 meters twice before it hits the ground. Find how many seconds since the launch would the objects height be 420 meters. Round your answers to two decimal places if needed.
The objects height would be 420 meters the first time at seconds, and then the second time at seconds.

solution.p: When the objects height is 420 meters, the equation becomes
We will change the equation to standard form:
Next, we can solve this quadratic equation with the Quadratic Formula. Identify that a=-4.9,b=100,c=-20.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned}[t] x \amp = \frac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^{2}-4ac}}{2a} \\ x \amp = \frac{-(100) \pm \sqrt{(100)^{2}-4(-4.9)(-20)}}{2(-4.9)} \\ x \amp = \frac{-100 \pm \sqrt{9608}}{-9.8} \\ x \amp \approx \frac{-100 \pm 98.0204060387428}{-9.8} \\ x\amp \approx\frac{-100 + 98.0204060387428}{-9.8} \text{ or } x\approx \frac{-100 - 98.0204060387428}{-9.8} \\ x\amp \approx0.2 \text{ or } x\approx20.21 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: The objects height would be 420 meters the first time at 0.2 seconds, and then the second time at 20.21 seconds.
